Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

It seems this patch is causing sanity test_415 to fail, as all the later patches are also failing. See:

 https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/query?utf8=✓&warn%5Bnotice%5D=&test_set_script_id=f9516376-32bc-11e0-aaee-52540025f9ae&sub_test_script_id=a765d5fa-7e82-11e8-acb8-52540065bddc&status%5B%5D=FAIL&horizon=1123200&commit=Update+results

Patch Set 3:

The failure of test_415 is not related to PCC, I think. There is a unresolved ticket about it: https://jira.whamcloud.com/browse/LU-11170 sanity test 415 fails with 'rename took N sec'.

Patch Set 3:

It is true that test 415 fails occasionally, but if you do the sub-test search for failures, there are 4 of 8-10 of the failures in the past few weeks are due to it or patches based on it. That is unlikely to be a random coincidence. 

One reason that this test might be failing more often is because this patch makes the code significantly slower.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(15 comments)

I looked briefly at test 415, and I'm curious to see if, with this updated & rebased, it keeps failing.  I agree with Quian that this doesn't look like it should cause that failure.  (That doesn't mean it's not causing it - But it seems unlikely.)

Patch Set 3:

I also haven't looked over the heat calculation in detail, I'll do that once it's broken out a bit.

Patch Set 3:

Yingjin,

Something we decided in Vancouver is we would like to get the file heat patch merged soon, before the rest of PCC.  The idea is that we might backport it to 2.12.  Are you able to update this one to respond to review comments soon?

commit.subject: LU-10602 llite: add file heat support
commit.message:
LU-10602 llite: add file heat support

File heat is a relative attribute fo files/objects which reflects
the access frequency of the files/objects.
File heat mainly desinged for cache management. Cache like PCC
could use file heat to determine which files to removed from
the cache or which files to fetch into cache.
This patch adds file heat support on llite level.

Signed-off-by: Li Xi <lixi@ddn.com>
Signed-off-by: Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I168fc657f0c859311e5114191b60047646909be0
